unit.title: Identity, Origin, And Family Basics
unit.communicationGoal: Say where you are from, name one or two family members, state a simple relationship, and introduce yourself and a family member from a short prompt.
pronunciationFocus: The ү in үй-бүлө is a front rounded vowel, not a plain u
Hear the -тен suffix in Бишкектенмин as one flowing ending
Менин runs quickly before the next word; do not pause between them

lexemes.notes: The question word for origin. Кайдансыз? means 'Where are you from?' in polite register. Кайдансың? is the informal equivalent.
The word for father. With the possessive suffix it becomes атам (my father). Ата is also used as a respectful address to an older man.
The word for mother. With the possessive suffix it becomes апам (my mother). Апа is also a warm address for an older woman.
Term for an older brother. Also used as a respectful address to any young-to-middle-aged man who is older than you. Байкем means 'my older brother'.
Term for an older sister. Also used as a polite address to any woman who is older than you but not yet elderly. Эжем means 'my older sister'.
Means both daughter and girl depending on context. Менин кызым means 'my daughter'. The ы is a back unrounded vowel, close to a muffled 'i'.
The common word for family as a household unit. Both vowels are front rounded (ü and ö). Туугандар is used for the wider circle of relatives.
The genitive form of мен (I). In Kyrgyz the noun after менин also takes a possessive suffix: Менин атам (my father). Both markers together is standard and not redundant.
A very common profession word. Менин апам мугалим — My mother is a teacher. No suffix needed on the predicate noun in a simple identity statement.
The capital of Kyrgyzstan. Useful in origin statements: Мен Бишкектенмин — I am from Bishkek. After Бишкек, the ablative suffix is -тен (not -ден) because к is voiceless.
The plural form for relatives. The root is тууган (kinsperson, born of). Туугандарым means 'my relatives'. Used when speaking about the wider family rather than the immediate household.
Kyrgyz has one third-person pronoun for all genders. Ал менин байкем means both 'He is my older brother' and 'She is my older sister' depending on context.

grammarNotes.title: Менин + noun + possessive suffix = my [noun]
grammarNotes.summary: To express 'my' in Kyrgyz, you use the pronoun менин in front of the noun AND add a short possessive suffix to the noun itself. Менин + ата + -м gives менин атам (my father). Both parts are needed — this double marking is normal and not a mistake. The suffix is -м after a vowel and -ым/-им after a consonant.

grammarNotes.title: The -дан / -ден / -тан / -тен suffix marks origin
grammarNotes.summary: To say where you are from, attach an ablative suffix to the place name and then add the copula -мин or -мын. Бишкек + -тен + -мин = Бишкектенмин (I am from Bishkek). Which form of the suffix? After a voiceless consonant (к, п, т) use -тан or -тен. After a voiced consonant or vowel use -дан or -ден. Pick тен vs тан based on the vowels in the word: front vowels get -тен, back vowels get -тан.

grammarNotes.title: Simple identity statements: noun + noun, no verb needed
grammarNotes.summary: In Kyrgyz, present-tense identity sentences like 'My father is a teacher' do not need a separate verb. Менин атам мугалим is a complete sentence. The subject (менин атам) sits next to the predicate (мугалим) and the meaning is clear. This is one of the most immediately useful patterns for talking about family and work.

tasks.objective: Introduce yourself and one family member using a short prompt card.
tasks.instructions: You have received a prompt card with a name, a home city, and one family member's name and job. Use the sentence patterns from this unit to introduce yourself and that family member. Speak or write at least four sentences.
successCriteria: State your name using the Менин атым pattern.
Say where you are from using the -тен / -ден + мин structure.
Name one family member and their relationship to you.
Say one fact about that family member (job, city, or name).
tasks.completionHint: Start with your name, then your city, then introduce the family member. Use Менин ... + family word + possessive suffix.

supportCards.title: Pattern reminder: origin statement
supportCards.body: Place name + -тен / -ден + мин. Example: Мен Ош шаарынантанмын — I am from the city of Osh. For Bishkek: Мен Бишкектенмин.

supportCards.title: Keep Менин short and flowing
supportCards.body: Менин is unstressed in a natural introduction. Let it run into the noun that follows without pausing. Menin atam sounds like one unit when spoken naturally.

tasks.scenario: A new neighbour is visiting your home for the first time. They ask about you and your family. Using the prompt card below, introduce yourself and one family member.
tasks.learnerRole: You are the host introducing yourself and your family.

reviewSeeds.prompt: What is the Kyrgyz word for 'mother'?
How do you say 'I am from Bishkek' in Kyrgyz?
In 'Менин атам', what does the -м ending on атам indicate?
